articles.title: The Iranian Lego AI video creators credit their virality to ‘heart’
articles.summary: Iranian content creators at Explosive Media have gone viral with AI-generated Lego videos depicting geopolitical events, including Trump and recent military incidents. The creators attribute their viral success to emotional authenticity and heart in their content.

articles.title: Fear and loathing at OpenAI
articles.summary: Sam Altman's tumultuous tenure at OpenAI, marked by his firing and reinstatement, raises concerns about his trustworthiness to lead transformative AI technology.

articles.title: Gen Z’s love-hate relationship with AI
articles.summary: Gen Z's enthusiasm for AI is declining as disillusionment grows, yet they continue using it in school and work. A Gallup survey shows cooling sentiment toward the technology despite feeling obligated to adopt it.

articles.title: Stalking victim sues OpenAI, claims ChatGPT fueled her abuser’s delusions and ignored her warnings
articles.summary: A stalking victim sued OpenAI, alleging ChatGPT fueled her abuser's delusions while the company ignored three warnings, including mass-casualty flags, about the dangerous user.

articles.title: TechCrunch is heading to Tokyo — and bringing the Startup Battlefield with it
articles.summary: TechCrunch's Startup Battlefield is coming to Tokyo for SusHi Tech 2026, focusing on AI, robotics, resilience, and entertainment with live demos and discussions on emerging technologies.

articles.title: Last 24 hours: Save up to $500 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass
articles.summary: TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 passes are on sale with discounts up to $500, ending tonight at 11:59 p.m. PT.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native infrastructure competing against AWS and Google Cloud.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code's $20-$200 monthly pricing sparks developer backlash as Goose, a free open-source alternative by Block, offers identical functionality without subscription fees or cloud dependency.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard hiring campaign using AI-encoded tokens and a coding challenge. The creative recruitment stunt successfully attracted top engineering talent in a competitive market.

articles.title: “Negative” views of Broadcom driving thousands of VMware migrations, rival says
articles.summary: Nutanix claims to have migrated approximately 30,000 VMware customers due to negative sentiment around Broadcom's VMware acquisition and strategy.

articles.title: Iran-linked hackers disrupt operations at US critical infrastructure sites
articles.summary: Iran-linked hackers are disrupting US critical infrastructure by targeting programmable logic controllers in factories, refineries, and water treatment facilities. Six US government agencies warn of ongoing attacks since March 2026.

articles.title: Thousands of consumer routers hacked by Russia's military
articles.summary: Russia's military hacked 18,000-40,000 consumer routers globally, redirecting users to credential-harvesting sites for espionage. APT28, part of Russia's GRU, targeted primarily MikroTik and TP-Link devices across 120 countries.

articles.title: What’s in a name? Moderna’s “vaccine” vs. “therapy” dilemma
articles.summary: Moderna faces vaccine skepticism in the Trump administration, losing contracts including a $776 million bird flu vaccine award. The company may pivot toward calling mRNA products "therapies" rather than "vaccines" to navigate regulatory challenges.

articles.title: The Download: an exclusive Jeff VanderMeer story and AI models too scary to release
articles.summary: Jeff VanderMeer's story 'Constellations' features a spacecraft crash on a hostile planet where three survivors and an AI must navigate alien domes connected by cables to survive. The article also discusses AI models considered too dangerous for public release.

articles.title: Constellations
articles.summary: A crash-landed spaceship crew explores a hostile alien planet with limited resources and a failed rescue beacon. They discover alien artifacts nearby, offering hope amid their dire survival situation.

articles.title: Waypoint-1.5: Higher-Fidelity Interactive Worlds for Everyday GPUs
articles.summary: Waypoint-1.5 enables higher-fidelity interactive 3D worlds on consumer GPUs. The advancement improves visual quality and performance for everyday hardware.

articles.title: Multimodal Embedding & Reranker Models with Sentence Transformers
articles.summary: Sentence Transformers now supports multimodal embedding and reranker models, enabling AI systems to process and compare text with images for improved semantic understanding and ranking accuracy.

articles.title: ALTK‑Evolve: On‑the‑Job Learning for AI Agents
articles.summary: ALTK-Evolve enables AI agents to learn and improve during real-world task execution. The system allows agents to adapt and evolve their capabilities while performing jobs.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of AI development and emerging trends in the field.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation and marketing operations.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft has open-sourced its 'farm of the future' toolkit, making agricultural AI technology publicly available for developers and farmers to improve farming practices.

articles.title: New ways to balance cost and reliability in the Gemini API
articles.summary: Google introduces two new inference tiers for the Gemini API: Flex and Priority, designed to balance cost and latency for better performance optimization.

articles.title: Create, edit and share videos at no cost in Google Vids
articles.summary: Google Vids introduces free AI-powered video creation and editing using Lyria 3 and Veo 3.1, enabling users to generate high-quality videos at no cost.

articles.title: We’re creating a new satellite imagery map to help protect Brazil’s forests.
articles.summary: Google partnered with the Brazilian government to create a satellite imagery map designed to protect Brazil's forests. The initiative uses advanced imaging technology to monitor and preserve forest ecosystems.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google releases Gemma 4, its most capable open-source models yet, specifically designed for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
articles.summary: Google's Gemini 3.1 Flash Live voice model improves audio AI with enhanced precision and lower latency for more natural and fluid voice interactions.

articles.title: Protecting people from harmful manipulation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind researches how AI systems could manipulate people in finance and health sectors, developing safety measures to prevent harmful exploitation.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and higher per-token prices, claiming improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family (MoE, 119B total parameters).

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia unveiled DLSS 5, a generative AI graphics technology that enhances photorealism in video games up to 4K by analyzing scene semantics and lighting to generate realistic details in real-time.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 discusses major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR pricing, Cursor's coding automations, and new interactive visualization features from ChatGPT and Anthropic.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, addressing debugging challenges in collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduces Astra, a dual-model architecture for robot navigation that addresses localization, destination identification, and path planning challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data and updating their own weights through learned self-editing processes via reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Why harness engineering is becoming the new AI moat
articles.summary: As LLMs become commoditized, the sophisticated engineering harness surrounding them emerges as the true competitive advantage. Anthropic's Claude Code leak demonstrates that implementation infrastructure, not just the base model, is becoming the real moat.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware exploits macOS binaries to steal credentials from developers running local AI agents on Mac Minis, targeting the growing OpenClaw AI agent ecosystem.

articles.title: Why Meta’s V-JEPA 2.1 model is a massive step forward for real-world AI
articles.summary: Meta's V-JEPA 2.1 advances video AI by balancing motion tracking with spatial detail through improved video self-supervised learning.

articles.title: NVIDIA Releases AITune: An Open-Source Inference Toolkit That Automatically Finds the Fastest Inference Backend for Any PyTorch Model
articles.summary: NVIDIA released AITune, an open-source inference toolkit that automatically optimizes PyTorch models for production by selecting the fastest backend without requiring custom engineering work.

articles.title: Five AI Compute Architectures Every Engineer Should Know: CPUs, GPUs, TPUs, NPUs, and LPUs Compared
articles.summary: Modern AI relies on diverse specialized processors including CPUs, GPUs, TPUs, NPUs, and LPUs, each optimized for different tasks. Understanding these architectures is essential for engineers as enterprises shift toward workload-specific compute optimization.

articles.title: An End-to-End Coding Guide to NVIDIA KVPress for Long-Context LLM Inference, KV Cache Compression, and Memory-Efficient Generation
articles.summary: NVIDIA's KVPress enables efficient long-context LLM inference through KV cache compression. The guide demonstrates practical implementation, comparing compression strategies and their performance impacts for memory-sensitive applications.

articles.title: This handy electric screwdriver is now 50% off on Amazon - how to snag the deal
articles.summary: The Fanttik S1 Pro electric screwdriver is available at 50% off on Amazon. Perfect for home repairs, DIY projects, and crafts.

articles.title: Home Depot's Ryobi yard work bundle packs a free power tool - how to get yours
articles.summary: Home Depot offers a Ryobi yard work bundle featuring a string trimmer and leaf blower, with a free power tool of your choice included.

articles.title: 'Finally, a boomless headset for the office': How Jabra made headphones I'd take to a boardroom
articles.summary: Jabra's redesigned Evolve3 75 is a hybrid headset optimized for business use, featuring a boom-free design suitable for professional boardroom settings.

articles.title: The Roadmap to Mastering Agentic AI Design Patterns
articles.summary: Article outlines design patterns for building effective agentic AI systems. Provides guidance on mastering autonomous AI agents and their implementation strategies.

articles.title: A Hands-On Guide to Testing Agents with RAGAs and G-Eval
articles.summary: This guide covers testing AI agents using RAGAs and G-Eval frameworks. These tools help evaluate agent performance and reliability in retrieval-augmented generation systems.

articles.title: Handling Race Conditions in Multi-Agent Orchestration
articles.summary: Race conditions occur when multiple agents simultaneously write to the same resource, causing nonsensical outputs. Proper synchronization and coordination mechanisms are essential for multi-agent systems.

articles.title: When Things Get Weird with Custom Calendars in Tabular Models
articles.summary: Calendar-based Time Intelligence in Power BI and Fabric Tabular models offers powerful possibilities but comes with pitfalls. Users must be aware of potential issues when implementing custom calendars.

articles.title: Why MLOps Retraining Schedules Fail — Models Don’t Forget, They Get Shocked
articles.summary: Calendar-based ML retraining fails because models don't forget predictably—they experience sudden performance shocks. A shock-detection approach offers practical solutions for production systems.

articles.title: A Guide to Voice Cloning on Voxtral with a Missing Encoder
articles.summary: The article explores reconstructing audio codes for Voxtral text-to-speech model to enable voice cloning without a traditional encoder.

articles.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
articles.summary: NVIDIA highlights physical AI breakthroughs during National Robotics Week, showcasing how robots are transforming industries. Advances in robot learning, simulation, and foundation models accelerate deployment from virtual training to real-world applications.

articles.title: Strength and Destiny Collide: ‘Samson: A Tyndalston Story’ Arrives in the Cloud
articles.summary: Samson: A Tyndalston Story by Liquid Swords is now available on GeForce NOW, allowing players to stream the mythic narrative game on multiple devices. The release is part of four new games added to the cloud platform this week.

articles.title: From RTX to Spark: NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI
articles.summary: Google and NVIDIA optimized Gemma 4 models for efficient local AI execution across devices from RTX PCs to data centers. These small, fast open models enable on-device agentic AI with real-time contextual awareness.

articles.title: Understanding Amazon Bedrock model lifecycle
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock regularly releases new foundation model versions with improved capabilities and safety. Understanding the model lifecycle is essential for planning and managing AI applications, with testing available before migration.

articles.title: The future of managing agents at scale: AWS Agent Registry now in preview
articles.summary: AWS Agent Registry, now in preview through Amazon Bedrock AgentCore, enables enterprises to discover, share, and reuse agents and tools across their organization, addressing challenges of visibility, governance, and preventing duplicate development work.

articles.title: Embed a live AI browser agent in your React app with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore introduces BrowserLiveView, a React component enabling real-time visibility into AI agent browsing actions. Users can now monitor autonomous web interactions directly within applications using just three lines of JSX.

articles.title: Running Large-Scale GPU Workloads on Kubernetes with Slurm
articles.summary: Slurm, an open-source cluster management system used by 65% of TOP500 supercomputers, enables large-scale GPU workloads on Kubernetes while preserving existing job scripts and scheduling policies.

articles.title: Cut Checkpoint Costs with About 30 Lines of Python and NVIDIA nvCOMP
articles.summary: NVIDIA's nvCOMP enables LLM checkpoint cost reduction using just 30 lines of Python code. The technique compresses massive model snapshots, significantly lowering storage expenses during large-scale training.

articles.title: How to Accelerate Protein Structure Prediction at Proteome-Scale
articles.summary: Proteins function through complex interactions rather than isolation. The article discusses accelerating protein structure prediction at proteome-scale, focusing on quaternary protein structures and their biological significance.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study examines how different generations view social media advertisements across various platforms, exploring their varying attitudes and relationships with ads.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to achieving net zero emissions by 2030, developing technology to reduce its carbon footprint and sharing these solutions publicly.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with a non-traditional background pioneered innovative XR technology, demonstrating how unconventional paths can lead to breakthrough developments in emerging tech fields.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers present methods for identifying feature interactions in Large Language Models at scale to improve interpretability and transparency. This work aims to make AI decision-making processes more understandable for safer, more trustworthy systems.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Researchers propose information-driven design methods for imaging systems that optimize how encoders map objects to images while accounting for noise corruption in measurements.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new reinforcement learning algorithm uses divide-and-conquer instead of temporal difference learning, addressing scalability challenges and improving performance on long-horizon tasks.
